title: Sum an Array
description: Given an array of numbers, return the sum of all elements. If the array is empty, return 0.
whatShouldHappen: Start with a running total of 0.
Visit every element in the array from left to right.
Add each element to the running total.
Return the total after the loop ends.
patternExplanation: The accumulator pattern initializes a variable before the loop and updates it on every iteration, producing a single result from a collection.

title: Find the Max
description: Given a non-empty array of numbers, return the largest value. Do not use Math.max.
whatShouldHappen: Assume the first element is the current maximum.
Loop through the remaining elements.
If an element is larger than the current maximum, update the maximum.
Return the maximum after the loop.
patternExplanation: Seed a variable with the first element, then scan the rest — update whenever a larger value is found. Works in a single pass without sorting.

title: Count Even Numbers
description: Given an array of integers, return how many of them are even.
whatShouldHappen: Initialize a counter to 0.
Loop through every element in the array.
If the element is divisible by 2 (num % 2 === 0), increment the counter.
Return the counter after the loop.
patternExplanation: A conditional counter increments only when a condition is met — combining a loop with an if-statement to tally matching elements.

title: FizzBuzz
description: Given a number n, return an array of strings from 1 to n. For multiples of 3 use "Fizz", multiples of 5 use "Buzz", multiples of both use "FizzBuzz", otherwise use the number as a string.
whatShouldHappen: Loop from 1 to n (inclusive).
Check divisibility by 15 first (both 3 and 5) — push "FizzBuzz".
Then check divisibility by 3 — push "Fizz".
Then check divisibility by 5 — push "Buzz".
Otherwise push the number converted to a string.
patternExplanation: Order your conditions from most specific (divisible by 15) to least specific — otherwise the 3 or 5 check will match before the combined case.

title: Find First Duplicate
description: Given an array of integers, return the first element that appears more than once. If no duplicate exists, return -1. The "first" duplicate is the one whose second occurrence appears earliest in the array.
whatShouldHappen: Use a Set to track elements you have already seen.
Loop through the array from left to right.
For each element, check if it is already in the Set.
If yes, return it immediately — it is the first duplicate.
Otherwise add it to the Set and continue.
Return -1 if the loop finishes without finding a duplicate.
patternExplanation: A Set tracks membership in O(1) time. Checking seen.has() before seen.add() on each element catches the first repeated value in a single linear pass.
